title: Contract: Forster & Kroeger Landscape Maintenance for Chipping Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or his designee to execute a contract
and any amendments, with Forster & Kroeger Landscape Maintenance to provide
Chipping Services for three years from March 1, 2025 until February 29, 2028 with the
option to extend for two additional one-year terms in the amount not to exceed
$1,400,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The FY25-26 Adopted Budget includes funding for vegetation management and fuel
reduction efforts from a variety of sources that is available for use toward the cost of
chipping services within the Fire Department (General Fund, Measure FF and/or UC
Settlement Fund). The Fire Department may pursue grants to offset the cost of this
service and upon grant approval, these funds would be added to the Department budget
through the Annual Appropriation Ordinance (AAO) process.
sections.current_situation:
The City conducted RFP Specification No. 25-11697-C with proposals due no later than
January 7, 2025. The City received four proposals in response to this RFP. The
selection committee, composed of subject matter experts from the Fire Department and
the Department of Parks, Recreation, and Waterfront, met to evaluate the proposals on
January 22, 2025. The criteria used to rate the proposals were Project Approach (20%),
Expertise and References (40%), Cost (20%), and Relevant Experience (20%). Forster
and Kroeger (F&K) was selected as the highest scoring vendor. The Contractor has
signed the City’s Nuclear Free Zone Disclosure Form, Oppressive States Compliance
Statement, Living Wage Certification Form, and the Sanctuary City Compliance
Statement.
The citywide chipping program is one way the City supports residents across Fire Zones
1, 2 and 3 to remove vegetation from their properties and create defensible space. The
chipping program removed over 3,000 cubic yards of vegetative fuel load from Berkeley
homes in 2024 alone.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

Contract - Forster & Kroeger Landscape Maintenance for Chipping Services CONSENT CALENDAR
February 25, 2025
F&K was selected as the leading vendor that has adequate staff and equipment to
service Berkeley and years of experience working directly with the event-based,
curbside chipping software used by the City. Additionally, F&K has the following
certifications and licenses: State Contractor’s License with a C-27 certification
(#748791); Small Business (Micro) certification, with a 721029 classification, by the
California Department of General Services (#18296); certification as a State Minority
Business Enterprise with the California Department of Transportation (#31098);
registration with the California Department of Industrial Relations (#1000027030).
sections.background:
F & K responded to a Request for Proposal and was selected after submitting the most
competitive bid. All chipping contractors in the area were eligible to bid on the project,
with submittals due by January 7, 2025. Requirements for a complete bid offer included
the ability to provide at least two, 3-person crews for work between Monday and
Thursday over a 28-week period between March and December. Contractors were also
required to submit detailed equipment lists that included multiple sized box trucks
ranging from 14 to 24 cubic yard capacity as well as being able to supply their own
chippers. This was necessary to ensure that there was enough equipment available to
provide daily service and access to small roads in the hills.
All contractors were required to supply proof of insurance and liability coverage and
agree to an onsite inspection of their service yard and equipment.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This program will support the removal and thinning of hazardous vegetation which will
create more defensible space and help prevent a wildfire from becoming an urban
structural conflagration. A smaller wildfire will generate a smaller volume of smoke if
one does ignite in or adjacent to the City. The chipped vegetation is processed as green
waste by the City of Berkeley transfer station.
sections.rationale:
It is critically important that this program and contractor are in place by late Winter 2025
for a successful delivery in Spring 2025. This contract will provide up to four-seasons of
service from F & K who has executed, and never had cancelled, similar contracts with
neighboring agencies.
sections.alternatives:
Purchasing the necessary apparatus, equipment and hiring numerous part-time
employees required to run the program. This would be significantly more expensive and
require a degree of management and supervision that the City does not have staff to
fulfill.
CONTACT PERSON
David Sprague, Fire Chief, (510) 981-3473
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT - FORSTER & KROEGER LANDSCAPE MAINTENANCE FOR CHIPPING
SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City conducted RFP Specification No. 25-11697-C with proposals due
no later than January 7, 2025, and
WHEREAS, the City received four proposals and a selection committee composed of
subject matter experts from the Fire Department and the Department of Parks,
Recreation, and Waterfront met to evaluate the proposals, and
WHEREAS, Forster and Kroeger (F&K) was selected as the highest scoring vendor.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with Forster &
Kroeger Landscape Maintenance to provide Chipping Services for two years from May
1, 2025 until April 30, 2027 with the option to extend for two additional one-year terms in
an amount not to exceed $1,400,000.
